content: How does Paul address Timothy in the opening of 1 Timothy?
explanation: 1 Timothy 1:2 — 'To Timothy, my true child in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.'
options: My true child in the faith

content: According to 1 Timothy 1:5, what is the aim of the charge?
explanation: 1 Timothy 1:5 — 'The aim of our charge is love that issues from a pure heart and a good conscience and a sincere faith.'
options: Love — from a pure heart, good conscience, sincere faith

content: According to 1 Timothy 1:15, what is the trustworthy saying deserving full acceptance?
explanation: 1 Timothy 1:15 — 'The saying is trustworthy and deserving of full acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am the foremost.'
options: God created all things from nothing
Moses gave the law to the people
Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners

content: In 1 Timothy 1:15, how does Paul describe himself among sinners?
explanation: 1 Timothy 1:15 — '...of whom I am the foremost.' A profound humility from an apostle.

content: According to 1 Timothy 2:1, for whom does Paul urge prayers?
explanation: 1 Timothy 2:1 — 'First of all, then, I urge that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all people.'

content: According to 1 Timothy 2:5, who is the one mediator between God and men?
explanation: 1 Timothy 2:5 — 'For there is one God, and there is one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.'

content: According to 1 Timothy 2:6, what did Christ give Himself as for all?
explanation: 1 Timothy 2:6 — '...who gave himself as a ransom for all, which is the testimony given at the proper time.'

content: According to 1 Timothy 3:2, what qualities must an overseer have?
explanation: 1 Timothy 3:2 — 'Therefore an overseer must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, sober-minded, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach.'
options: Above reproach, husband of one wife, sober-minded, hospitable, able to teach

content: According to 1 Timothy 3:15, how is the church described?
explanation: 1 Timothy 3:15 — '...the church of the living God, a pillar and buttress of the truth.'
options: A pillar and buttress of the truth

content: According to 1 Timothy 3:16, how does the mystery of godliness begin?
explanation: 1 Timothy 3:16 — 'Great indeed, we confess, is the mystery of godliness: He was manifested in the flesh, vindicated by the Spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among the nations, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.'

content: According to 1 Timothy 4:1, what will some depart from the faith to follow in later times?
explanation: 1 Timothy 4:1 — 'Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith by devoting themselves to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons.'
options: Deceitful spirits and teachings of demons

content: According to 1 Timothy 4:8, for what is godliness profitable?
explanation: 1 Timothy 4:8 — '...for while bodily training is of some value, godliness is of value in every way, as it holds promise for the present life and also for the life to come.'
options: Profitable only for the life to come
Profitable in every way — for this life and the life to come

content: According to 1 Timothy 4:12, in what areas must Timothy set an example for believers?
explanation: 1 Timothy 4:12 — 'Let no one despise you for your youth, but set the believers an example in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.'
options: Ability to perform miracles and prophecy

content: According to 1 Timothy 4:13, to what must Timothy devote himself?
explanation: 1 Timothy 4:13 — 'Until I come, devote yourself to the public reading of Scripture, to exhortation, to teaching.'
options: Public reading of Scripture, exhortation, teaching

content: According to 1 Timothy 5:8, what about the one who does not provide for his household?
explanation: 1 Timothy 5:8 — 'But if anyone does not provide for his relatives, and especially for members of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.'
options: Will be helped by church leaders
Has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever
Will be held accountable in court

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:6, godliness with what is great gain?
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:6 — 'But godliness with contentment is great gain.'

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:10, what is the root of all kinds of evils?
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:10 — 'For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils. It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.'
tags: Love of money root of evils

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:11, what must the man of God pursue?
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:11 — 'But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness.'
options: Righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness
tags: Six virtues of man of God

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:12, what good fight must Timothy fight?
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:12 — 'Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and about which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.'
options: The good fight of the faith

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:15, how is God exalted?
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:15 — '...he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords.'
options: King of kings and Lord of lords

content: According to 1 Timothy 1:17, how is God described?
explanation: 1 Timothy 1:17 — 'To the King of the ages, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.'
options: King of the ages, immortal, invisible, the only God

content: According to 1 Timothy 3:2-3, which qualities must an overseer have? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Timothy 3:2-3 — '...above reproach, the husband of one wife, sober-minded, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach, not a drunkard, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money.' 'Eager for political power' is not in the list.
options: Above reproach, husband of one wife
Sober-minded, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach
Gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money

content: According to 1 Timothy 3:16, by what does the mystery of godliness describe Christ? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Timothy 3:16 — '...He was manifested in the flesh, vindicated by the Spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among the nations, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.' Six elements; 'elected by men as Christ' contradicts His being revealed by God.
options: Manifested in flesh, vindicated by Spirit
Seen by angels, proclaimed among the nations
Believed on in the world, taken up in glory
Elected by men as the Christ
tags: Six features of mystery of godliness

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:9-10, what consequences flow from the desire for riches? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:9-10 — '...those who desire to be rich fall into temptation, into a snare, into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction... some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.' Three consequences; 'multiplied material blessings' contradicts the passage.
options: Fall into temptation and a snare
Wander from faith, pierce themselves with pangs
Receive multiplied material blessings from God

content: According to 1 Timothy 6:11, what virtues must the man of God pursue? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Timothy 6:11 — '...Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness.' Six holy virtues; 'wealth and earthly honor' is precisely what must be fled.
tags: Six virtues of man of God
